meaning: n.[动]山魈(西非洲产的大狒狒)
examples: He's actually a mix between a mandrill and a baboon, as mandrills don't have tails.
A baboon ( Papio leucophaeus ) of western Africa, related to and resembling the mandrill .
Old World monkeys: guenon; baboon; colobus monkey; langur; macaque; mandrill; mangabey; patas; proboscis monkey.
The rocky great-apes enclosure is constantly surrounded by tittering schoolkids, pointing at the pink and blue posteriors on display in the baboon and mandrill pens.
